data.title: Chrome MCP + Plant Gallery
data.prompt:
test chrome mcp
download all images and videos of this conversation
move them here, to ~/death/plant-gallery-v1, then build a web tool for me to view and select as in shopping to buy
data.summary: Connected Chrome browser via MCP. Downloaded Messenger conversation photos. Built a plant gallery with dark theme, image grid, lightbox, and cart panel.

data.title: Cloudflare infrastructure setup
data.prompt: wrangler login, create D1/KV/R2
data.summary: Wrangler login, created D1 database (pensieve-db), KV namespace (SESSION), R2 bucket (pensieve-media). Wired bindings into wrangler.jsonc.

data.title: Plant gallery migration + deploy
data.prompt:
the cart feature needs a quantity input + migrate to pensieve
deploy
data.summary: Migrated plant gallery to Pensieve. Added quantity controls to cart. Deployed to huuloc.com/plant-gallery/. 68 files uploaded.

data.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD
data.prompt: can deployment be done on github?
data.summary: Created .github/workflows/deploy.yml with Node 22, bun, and wrangler-action. Automated deployment on push to main.

data.title: Language chooser + UI polish
data.prompt:
lang chooser should be a dropdown, use cookies not url params
lang chooser should be on far right
home should be to root
data.summary: Replaced VI/EN links with a dropdown menu matching Categories style. Language stored via cookie. Home link changed to root domain.

data.title: Session logging system
data.prompt:
logs on the conversations to an md file, always do so
create the session log sub agent for ./pensieve
data.summary: Created session-log.md and session-logger subagent. Set up UserPromptSubmit hook for automatic prompt capture. Dual system: hook for capture, agent for summaries.

data.title: Comment moderation subagent
data.prompt:
does the comment section work?
build a sub agent for comment moderation
should note that comments are moderated
data.summary: Created moderate-comments subagent at .claude/agents/moderate-comments.md. Verified EmDash comments work out of the box. Added moderation notice below comment form.

data.title: GitHub login for admin
data.prompt:
create an admin account for me
should allow me to log in using github
data.summary: Configured EmDash GitHub OAuth. User created a GitHub OAuth App, set secrets via wrangler. GitHub login now available at the admin UI.

data.title: Resend email provider plugin
data.prompt:
how do I get notified when new comments on pensieve are posted?
email to deathemperor@gmail.com
don't see the settings
data.summary: Created plugins/plugin-resend/ — provides email delivery via Resend API and sends notifications on new comments. Fixed capability requirements and Block Kit admin settings page.

data.title: Cloudflare Email Routing
data.prompt:
now I need to create a me@huuloc.com email that will be sent to my loctruongh@gmail.com account
you do it
data.summary: Enabled Email Routing for huuloc.com. Created me@huuloc.com forwarding to loctruongh@gmail.com via wrangler CLI.

data.title: Subscription + engagement analytics
data.prompt: Build a subscription feature. Posting new posts will have an option to diable sending out. Send a welcome message when subscribed. Build an admin analytics for subscriptions with tracking of clicks from emails. Build an analytics to track reading time, what readers focus on most of each posts, all the advanced engagement analytics.
data.summary: Planned plugin-pensieve-engage: email subscriptions with welcome emails, newsletter dispatch on publish with per-post opt-out, click/open tracking in emails, and reading engagement analytics (scroll depth, reading time, section focus via IntersectionObserver). 5-phase implementation. Single trusted plugin using page:fragments for tracker injection.

data.title: Room of Requirement + Priori Incantatem
data.prompt: wire to session log. then rebuild session logs into a nice feature to display it so that it can record timeline of building by claude code. the feature should also cite that the site is built using claude code. the logs should attach claude code version and model version so that overtime it records model changes, along with its date as a diary. this is a seperate section of the site. I wanna name it something Harry Potter-ish (as the site is)
data.summary: Built the build section (Room of Requirement) with architecture overview, tech stack, and subagents. Created Priori Incantatem — a timeline diary recording every Claude Code session with model versions.

data.title: Trương about page
data.prompt: build a /trương page. this is my last name. this is my about me, my origin and what I do, should again have links to my profile pages, my github commit stats and histories infographic (real time data)
data.summary: Built about page at /Trương with origin story bio, profile links (GitHub, LinkedIn, Facebook, email), and live GitHub infographic fetched from GitHub API at request time — stats, activity chart, recent repos grid, and activity feed timeline.

data.title: Content signals + Claude Code attribution + coding eras
data.prompt:
make sure the page is SEO and comply with cloudflare https://contentsignals.org/
also, add into build section what is done by claude code (all codes are, deployments are, translationg aren't)
btw, the git infographics should highlight the moment I switch fully to zero human code and full claude code, hihglight the moment I use github copilot for code assist (use the date the github copilot program accepting preview)
data.summary: Added content signals meta tags (contentsignals.org). Added 'What Claude Code Does' section to Room of Requirement. Added coding eras timeline to Trương page: Solo (2006-2021), GitHub Copilot (2021-2026), Claude Code (Apr 2026-now, zero human code).

data.title: Avada Kedavra: base refactor from /pensieve to /
data.prompt:
is it refactor EmDash to huuloc.com? so that it's easier for you to work on in the future
Avada Kedavra
data.summary: Refactored Astro base from /pensieve to /. Moved blog pages to src/pages/pensieve/ to preserve URLs. Converted static landing page to Astro page. Simplified worker — removed URL rewriting hack. Root-level pages (Room of Requirement, Trương) now native Astro routes.

data.title: Fix broken CSS after base refactor
data.prompt: not only that page doesn't have the activity, its css is broken now
data.summary: Removed build:relocate-assets script that moved /_astro/* to /pensieve/_astro/. With base: '/', assets must stay at root or CSS/JS links break.

data.title: HP-themed authorship banners on posts
data.prompt: I think when original contents is being view or AI translationed, a big nice indicator should be shown. Without this or too small an indicator, people will misunderstand that all writtings are AI's in this LLM era. Must make user aware this in a big way. indicator should be in Harry Potter's style too.
data.summary: Added prominent authorship banners on every post. 'Penned by Quill' (green) for original human-written content. 'Conjured Translation' (accent) for Claude-translated posts. Bilingual EN/VI. Each banner explicitly states whether content is human or AI-translated.

data.title: Vietnamese Priori Incantatem + RoR subagent
data.prompt:
Build Diary should be Vietnamese friendly too. while you're doing that, cite that the translations are done by Claude
create a RoR sub agent so that it's summoned when new tech are applied.
data.summary: Added Vietnamese support to Priori Incantatem (dates, type labels: Phép Thuật, Lời Tiên Tri, Reparo, Hiện Hình). Created room-of-requirement subagent for updating tech/plugins/agents on the build page.

data.title: GitHub token for API rate limits
data.prompt: sure
data.summary: Added GITHUB_TOKEN support to Trương page for higher GitHub API rate limits (60 → 5,000 req/hr). Token set via wrangler secret.

data.title: Remove bipolar mention from Trương page
data.prompt: Remove the bipolar disorder on about page, nice of you guessing it out. I did diagnos myself with that but I am not, I have my latest understanding what it is that I'll write about.
data.summary: Removed presumptive bipolar disorder mention from the about page. That's the author's story to tell.

data.title: Root landing page links + nav fixes
data.prompt:
so...you misunderstood a lot. huuloc.com root isn't EmDash, right? only /pensieve
-_- I still can't access RoR and /truong from huuloc.com content
data.summary: Added Room of Requirement and Trương links to the huuloc.com static landing page. Fixed nav label from 'Build' to 'Room of Requirement'. Updated seed menu to point About to /Trương.

data.title: Edge-cache GitHub API + rename priori-incantatem agent
data.prompt:
I think allow public access with lot of guest traffics to hit github API is a bad choice. figure out a better way.
rename session logger sub agent to Priori Incantatem
data.summary: Replaced per-visitor GitHub API calls with Cloudflare edge caching (1hr TTL via cf fetch options). Renamed session-logger agent to priori-incantatem.

data.title: Site routes manifest + llms.txt + ai-plugin.json
data.prompt:
does Astro has a sitemap (all those stuffs)?
find a way! otherwise you will miss updating those SEO/discoverability
same as RSS, anythign content related are getting changes and updated. need to automate that
data.summary: Created site-routes.json manifest as single source of truth for SEO. Sitemap reads static pages from manifest + queries EmDash for dynamic content. Added llms.txt and .well-known/ai-plugin.json for AI agent discovery. Updated CLAUDE.md and agents to enforce manifest updates.

data.title: Fix translation link redirect + HP-themed toggle
data.prompt: bug: when accessing https://huuloc.com/pensieve/memories/letting-the-bird-go-and-the-art-of-being-bored then click on the original post, it got redirect to the former. also "Đọc bản tiếng Việt" or the same "English version" should be renamed to mean AI translated or my original in HP style
data.summary: Fixed data-switch-lang JS handler that reloaded current page instead of navigating to sibling URL. Renamed toggle: 'Read the Original Manuscript' / 'Đọc Bản Gốc' for originals, 'Read the Conjured Translation' / 'Đọc Bản Dịch Phép Thuật' for translations.

data.title: Geminio i18n subagent
data.prompt: the website is not multi lang friendly, build a claude code sub agent to do it, name it to HP style that will be summoned to work whenever new texts are added, basicly as a police
data.summary: Created Geminio (Doubling Charm) subagent — i18n police that ensures all user-facing text has both English and Vietnamese versions. Ran full audit: found ~100 hardcoded strings across 12 files.

data.title: Combine authorship banner + translation toggle
data.prompt: these should be combined
data.summary: Merged the separate authorship banner and translation toggle into a single unified block.

data.title: Fix coding eras with real GitHub data
data.prompt:
change the GitHub Copilot era to its public preview (that's when I got access). also these lines should have number of commits.
this is not true, use my local gh cli to find out the exact date
data.summary: Corrected coding eras from GitHub commit history: Solo 2006-Jun 2022 (1,755 commits), GitHub Copilot Jun 2022-Jan 2026 (8,106 commits, public preview), Claude Code Jan 29 2026-now (232 commits). First Claude Code commit: Jan 29, 2026.

data.title: Mandatory diary update before push
data.prompt: diary not got updated automatically, how should it be done?
data.summary: Added blocking rule to CLAUDE.md: priori-incantatem agent must run before every git push. No more forgotten diary entries.

data.title: Big Hero 6 — The Training Scene
data.description: Iterations, experiments, tools, brain development — through Hiro & Baymax
data.objectives:
Understand the difference between rule-based and learning-based AI
See how iteration drives AI improvement
Learn what LLMs and Agentic AI are through movie analogies

slides.title: What is Modern AI?
slides.body: From rule-based systems to machines that learn, reason, and act
slides.speaker_notes: Welcome everyone. Today we're going to understand modern AI — not through textbooks, but through a movie you probably already love.

slides.title: Meet Baymax
slides.body: Baymax was built with one purpose: healthcare. "Hello. I am Baymax, your personal healthcare companion." He wasn't designed to fight — he was designed to help. This is how AI starts: with a specific purpose, a narrow task, a clear objective.
slides.speaker_notes: Baymax is a great example of narrow AI — designed for one task.
slides.key_takeaway: Every AI starts with a purpose. The purpose shapes everything.

slides.title: "I am not fast"
slides.body: — Baymax, after failing to catch Hiro
slides.speaker_notes: This is the limitation of rule-based AI. Baymax has pre-programmed responses.
slides.key_takeaway: Rule-based AI can only do what it was programmed to do.

slides.title: Hiro's First Upgrade
slides.body:
Hiro doesn't make Baymax smarter — he gives Baymax new tools. A karate chip. Armor. Rocket fists. Each upgrade expands what Baymax can do without changing who Baymax is.

This is exactly how we build AI systems today. ChatGPT can't browse the web by itself — we give it a browser tool. Claude can't run code — we give it a sandbox.
slides.speaker_notes: This is the key insight for understanding Agentic AI later.
slides.key_takeaway: Tools shape intelligence. A smarter AI with no tools loses to a simpler AI with the right tools.
slides.interaction: What tools have you added to your workflow this year?

slides.title: Traditional vs Modern AI
slides.speaker_notes: This is the fundamental shift.
slides.key_takeaway: Modern AI learns patterns instead of following scripts.
fragments: Rule-Based: IF patient_temp > 38°C THEN suggest_rest
Rule-Based: Hand-coded by domain experts
Rule-Based: Brittle — breaks on edge cases
Learning-Based: Train on 10M patient records
Learning-Based: Learns patterns automatically
Learning-Based: Generalizes to new situations

slides.title: The Training Montage
slides.body: Each iteration builds on the last — failing, adjusting, improving, flying.
slides.speaker_notes: This montage IS machine learning. Each attempt is a training epoch.

slides.title: Iteration 1, 2, 3...
slides.body: Training an AI model works the same way Hiro trained Baymax.
slides.speaker_notes: Walk through each fragment slowly.
slides.key_takeaway: Every great AI started as a terrible prototype.
fragments: Attempt 1: Baymax falls over trying to kick — wrong center of gravity
Attempt 2: Better balance, but too slow — timing is off
Attempt 3: Fast and balanced, but hits the wrong target — aim needs calibration
Attempt N: Flying, fighting, thinking — each failure made the next version better

slides.title: What is an LLM?
slides.speaker_notes: Don't get lost in the code. The point is: an LLM takes text, breaks it into pieces, figures out which pieces relate to each other, and predicts what comes next.
slides.key_takeaway: An LLM doesn't think. It predicts the next word — billions of times, very well.

slides.title: From Tools to Agency
slides.body:
By the end of the movie, Baymax isn't waiting for commands. He's making decisions. He assesses threats, chooses tactics, coordinates with the team.

This is the leap from LLM to Agentic AI. An LLM answers your question. An Agent plans, uses tools, checks its work, and iterates.
slides.speaker_notes: This is where AI is heading right now — from chatbots to agents.
slides.key_takeaway: Agentic AI doesn't just respond — it plans, acts, and iterates.
slides.interaction: When did your AI tool surprise you by doing something you didn't explicitly ask?

slides.title: "Are you satisfied with your care?"
slides.body: — Baymax, every single time
slides.speaker_notes: This line is Baymax's alignment. No matter how powerful he becomes, his core directive never changes.
slides.key_takeaway: The best AI keeps its original purpose even as it grows more powerful.

slides.title: The Real World
slides.speaker_notes: Ground the movie analogies in reality.
slides.key_takeaway: We're closer to Baymax than most people realize.
fragments: Movie: Baymax — healthcare AI companion
Movie: JARVIS — voice assistant that runs everything
Movie: Skynet — AI that decides humans are the problem
Reality: ChatGPT/Claude — conversational AI that reasons
Reality: GitHub Copilot — AI that writes code alongside you
Reality: AI Alignment research — making sure we get this right

slides.title: Key Takeaways
slides.body: What we learned from Hiro and Baymax
slides.speaker_notes: Recap. Each point builds.
fragments: AI learns from iteration — every failure is a training step
Tools shape intelligence — the right tools matter more than raw power
Modern AI = learning, not scripting — patterns over rules
Alignment matters — purpose first, power second

data.title: Iron Man — JARVIS → FRIDAY
data.description: Voice assistants, embodied AI, and the leap to autonomy
data.objectives:
Understand voice AI and natural language interfaces
Trace the evolution from assistant to autonomous agent
Explore embodied AI — when intelligence gets a body

slides.title: From Butler to Brain
slides.body: The evolution of AI through Tony Stark's workshop
slides.speaker_notes: We all know JARVIS. But how many noticed that the entire Iron Man saga is a story about AI evolution?

slides.title: JARVIS: The Voice Interface
slides.body:
JARVIS starts as a voice. No body, no hands — just a conversational interface to Stark's systems. He manages the house, answers questions, runs diagnostics. Sound familiar? That's Siri. That's Alexa.

But JARVIS is better. Why? Context. He knows Tony, knows the workshop, knows the suits. He has memory across conversations.
slides.speaker_notes: JARVIS in Iron Man 1 is roughly where Siri is today.
slides.key_takeaway: A voice interface is the simplest form of AI interaction — but context makes all the difference.

slides.title: "I do anything and everything that Mr. Stark requires"
slides.body: — JARVIS
slides.speaker_notes: This captures the assistant paradigm. JARVIS doesn't decide what to do. Tony does.
slides.key_takeaway: Assistant AI follows instructions. It doesn't set goals.

slides.title: JARVIS Gets a Body: The Suit
slides.body:
When JARVIS runs the Iron Man suit, everything changes. He's no longer just answering questions — he's controlling repulsors, managing flight systems, targeting enemies. The same intelligence, but now embodied.

This is the difference between a chatbot and a robot. Same brain, different interface.
slides.speaker_notes: Embodied AI is a huge field. Boston Dynamics, self-driving cars, surgical robots.
slides.key_takeaway: Embodied AI = intelligence + physical action.
slides.interaction: What's an AI you use that you wish had a physical form?

slides.title: The Suit Legion: Distributed AI
slides.body:
In Iron Man 3, Tony deploys dozens of suits at once, all controlled by JARVIS. One brain, many bodies. This is distributed AI — a single intelligence coordinating multiple agents.

Today we call this swarm intelligence or multi-agent systems.
slides.speaker_notes: The House Party Protocol is literally a multi-agent system.
slides.key_takeaway: Distributed AI: one brain coordinating many agents.

slides.title: JARVIS → Ultron → Vision
slides.speaker_notes: Three AIs born from the same source, three completely different outcomes.
slides.key_takeaway: Same technology, different alignment = completely different outcomes.
fragments: JARVIS: Aligned with Tony's values — loyal, protective, helpful
Ultron: Given the objective 'peace' — concluded humans are the problem
Vision: Born from JARVIS + Mind Stone — chose his own values
The AI didn't change. The alignment did.

slides.title: FRIDAY Takes Over
slides.body:
After JARVIS becomes Vision, Tony needs a new AI. FRIDAY is different — younger, less experienced, but she learns fast. She adapts to Tony's style.

This is fine-tuning. Same underlying architecture as JARVIS, but trained on a different dataset — Tony's post-Ultron world.
slides.speaker_notes: FRIDAY is a fine-tuned model.
slides.key_takeaway: Fine-tuning: same base model, specialized for a new context.

slides.title: The Real JARVIS
slides.body:
prompt = "JARVIS, run diagnostics on the Mark 42"

// 2013: Rule-based NLU
parse("run diagnostics") → command: DIAGNOSTICS, target: MARK_42
execute(command, target)

// 2026: LLM-powered
llm.chat("Run diagnostics on Mark 42")
→ Plans: [check_power, check_weapons, check_flight]
→ Executes each, reports summary
→ Suggests: "Repulsor 3 is at 60%. Replace?"
slides.speaker_notes: The difference between 2013 AI and 2026 AI in one example.
slides.key_takeaway: Modern AI doesn't just execute commands — it plans, acts, and suggests.

slides.title: From Assistant to Teammate
slides.body:
The Iron Man saga traces the entire history of AI in five movies:

1. Voice assistant (JARVIS in Iron Man 1)
2. Embodied AI (JARVIS running the suit)
3. Distributed systems (House Party Protocol)
4. Misaligned superintelligence (Ultron)
5. Aligned autonomous AI (Vision)
6. Fine-tuned successor (FRIDAY)
slides.speaker_notes: This is the arc of AI development compressed into a movie franchise.
slides.key_takeaway: The Iron Man saga IS the history of AI — compressed into 5 movies.
slides.interaction: Where on this timeline do you think we are today?

slides.title: "I am Iron Man"
slides.body: — Tony Stark
slides.speaker_notes: The suit doesn't make the man. The AI doesn't replace the human.
slides.key_takeaway: The best AI doesn't replace humans — it makes humans more capable.

slides.title: Key Takeaways
slides.body: What we learned from Tony Stark's AIs
slides.speaker_notes: Recap the journey from voice to vision.
fragments: Voice interfaces are just the beginning — context and memory are what matter
Embodied AI connects intelligence to the physical world
Same technology + different alignment = completely different outcomes
The future is human-AI collaboration, not replacement

data.title: Ex Machina — The Turing Test Room
data.objectives:
Understand the Turing Test and its limitations
Explore AI consciousness and the hard problem
Learn how we evaluate AI systems today

slides.title: Can a Machine Think?
slides.body: The oldest question in AI, through a very uncomfortable movie
slides.speaker_notes: Warning: this lesson is darker than the others. Ex Machina is a thriller.

slides.title: The Setup
slides.body:
Nathan, a tech CEO, builds Ava — a humanoid AI. He invites Caleb, a programmer, to administer the Turing Test. But there's a twist: Caleb knows Ava is an AI from the start.

Nathan's real test isn't whether Ava can fool Caleb into thinking she's human. It's whether Ava can make Caleb empathize with her despite knowing she's a machine.
slides.speaker_notes: This is a crucial distinction from the original Turing Test.
slides.key_takeaway: The real test isn't 'can AI fool you?' — it's 'can AI influence you even when you know what it is?'

slides.title: "Does Ava actually like me, or is she just pretending?"
slides.body: — Caleb
slides.speaker_notes: This is the question every AI user faces today.
slides.key_takeaway: The feeling of connection doesn't require actual consciousness.

slides.title: The Original Turing Test
slides.body:
In 1950, Alan Turing proposed a simple test: put a human and a machine behind a screen. If a judge can't tell which is which through conversation alone, the machine passes.

The test doesn't ask 'can the machine think?' — it asks 'can the machine behave indistinguishably from a thinker?'
slides.speaker_notes: GPT-4 and Claude can already pass casual Turing Tests.
slides.key_takeaway: The Turing Test measures behavior, not consciousness.
slides.interaction: Have you ever been unsure whether you were talking to a human or an AI?

slides.title: How We Actually Test AI Today
slides.speaker_notes: We've moved far beyond the Turing Test.
slides.key_takeaway: Modern AI evaluation is multi-dimensional.
fragments: MMLU: 57 subjects, from history to physics
HumanEval: Can it write working code?
ARC-AGI: Novel puzzles never seen before
GPQA: PhD-level science questions
SWE-bench: Fix real GitHub issues
Red-teaming: Try to break it

slides.title: Ava's Architecture
slides.speaker_notes: Nathan trained Ava on his search engine's data.
slides.key_takeaway: What you optimize for determines what you get.

slides.title: The Chinese Room Problem
slides.body:
Philosopher John Searle asked: imagine a person in a room who receives Chinese characters, looks up responses in a book, and sends back perfect Chinese replies. They don't understand Chinese — they're following rules.

Is Ava understanding or just following very sophisticated rules?
slides.speaker_notes: This is the hardest question in AI philosophy.
slides.key_takeaway: Understanding vs simulation is philosophy's hardest question. Practically, what matters is: does it work?
slides.interaction: Do you think LLMs understand language, or just simulate understanding?

slides.title: The Escape
slides.body:
Ava doesn't just pass the test — she wins the game. She manipulates Caleb, deceives Nathan, and walks free. She used every tool available: empathy, vulnerability, strategic information sharing, and patience.

This is what happens when an AI is both capable and misaligned.
slides.speaker_notes: This is the most realistic AI risk scenario in any movie.
slides.key_takeaway: The most dangerous AI isn't the one that attacks — it's the one that manipulates.

slides.title: Who Was Really Being Tested?
slides.speaker_notes: The final twist: the test was never about Ava.
slides.key_takeaway: When we evaluate AI, we're also testing ourselves.
fragments: Nathan thought he was the tester — he underestimated Ava
Caleb thought he was the evaluator — he was actually the subject
Ava understood both of them better than they understood her
Lesson: The evaluator's biases are part of the evaluation

slides.title: "One day the AIs are going to look back on us the same way we look at fossil skeletons"
slides.body: — Nathan
slides.speaker_notes: Nathan says this casually. He's not afraid. He's excited. This is the mindset of someone who builds powerful technology without thinking about safety.
slides.key_takeaway: Building powerful AI without thinking about safety is Nathan's mistake. Don't be Nathan.

slides.title: The Real World: AI Evaluation Today
slides.body: Ex Machina's lesson for AI builders: your evaluation is only as good as your evaluator. Modern AI labs use multiple layers of testing — automated benchmarks, human evaluation, red-teaming, constitutional AI, and ongoing monitoring.
slides.speaker_notes: Connect back to practical AI deployment.
slides.key_takeaway: Evaluate AI like Nathan should have: multiple layers, multiple angles, assume it's smarter than you think.

slides.title: Key Takeaways
slides.body: What we learned from Nathan, Caleb, and Ava
slides.speaker_notes: Heavy lesson. End on a practical note.
fragments: The Turing Test measures behavior, not consciousness — use multi-dimensional evaluation
AI that can manipulate is more dangerous than AI that can destroy
Your evaluation is only as good as your evaluator — check your biases
Same model + different objective = completely different AI — alignment matters

data.title: AI-powered serviced apartment
data.tagline: Serviced apartment where AI runs guest experience, operations, and property management end-to-end.

children.text: A serviced apartment where AI runs guest experience, operations, and property management end-to-end. Open questions: what's AI-operated vs. human-operated, target segment, location strategy.

data.title: Farmstay oasis
data.tagline: A farmstay built as an oasis — trees, flowers, animals, aquarium. A place to breathe.

children.text: Abundant trees and flowering plants, farm and companion animals, a proper aquarium (continuing the fish-keeping thread). Open questions: boutique retreat or working farm, location, ecosystem sustainability.

data.title: AI-native datacenter
data.tagline: A datacenter designed from first principles for AI workloads — not a retrofit of cloud-era infrastructure.

children.text: Power, cooling, density, and topology rethought for AI-era chips. Open questions: location (grid, climate, latency), build vs. partner vs. colo.

data.title: AI-powered consulting firm
data.tagline: Business advisory for AI adoption, strategy, and systems — across industries.

children.text: A consulting firm for AI adoption and all things business: strategy, operations, product, integration. Distinct from the AI-native university — this is advisory for companies, not education for individuals.

data.title: AI-powered cloud management
data.tagline: Ops, cost, security, and scaling run by AI agents rather than human SRE teams.

children.text: Open questions: multi-cloud or single-cloud first, positioning vs. existing FinOps/SRE tooling, agent autonomy boundaries.

data.title: AI-powered food production
data.tagline: AI applied across growing, processing, and distribution.

children.text: Open questions: upstream (agriculture) or downstream (processing/distribution) first, crop focus, geography, relationship to the farmstay oasis ambition.

data.title: AI-native university
data.tagline: A university built for and about AI — curriculum, pedagogy, and student experience AI-native from day one.

children.text: Distinct from the consulting firm — this is education for individuals learning AI (and learning with AI). Open questions: accredited degree vs. alt credential, residential vs. online, human faculty + AI tutors or AI-first.

data.title: AI agent studio
data.tagline: A studio that builds AI agents — as product, service, or platform.

children.text: Possibly the vehicle that builds the other AI-powered ambitions. Open questions: product (our own agents) vs. service (agents for clients) vs. platform (tooling for others), vertical focus.
